headline: Hungary enters new era following Orban's defeat
subhead: During a three-hour press conference, Péter Magyar, the conservative yet pro-European winner of the election, maintained a degree of ambiguity on the subject of Ukraine but outlined a return to the rule of law for the small Central European country.

headline: American Catholics outraged at Trump's attack of pope and AI-generated image of himself as Christ
One year ahead of French presidential election, survey shows deep desire for change
Trump vows US to 'eliminate' any Iranian ships that approach blockade
subhead: The US military enforced a blockade around Iran's ports on Monday evening.

headline: Reza Pahlavi's controversial visit to Sweden's Parliament
subhead: The eldest son of Iran's last shah was received by MPs in Stockholm on Monday. His visit sparked excitement among some Iranians in exile, but also anger from those who do not recognize his legitimacy.

headline: Restitution of looted cultural artifacts: Assemblée Nationale approves amended legislation
subhead: Following the Sénat, the Assemblée Nationale unanimously passed this long-awaited bill on Monday, April 13. Amended by several revisions, the legislation will now be examined in a joint committee to harmonize positions between both chambers.

headline: Jordan Bardella, a princess and 'Paris Match': The story of a PR gamble
subhead: The relationship between Marine Le Pen's lieutenant and an aristocratic student raised in the international jet set made the front page of 'Paris Match.' The media empire of Vincent Bolloré has seized the opportunity to 'glamorize' the likely far-right presidential candidate.

subhead: The crowded field of Democratic candidates running in the gubernatorial election raises the possibility that a Trump-aligned Republican could win in November in this deeply progressive state.

headline: Parenting: Why the morning rush drives us crazy
subhead: Le Monde's parenting columnist, Clara Georges, breaks down one of her typically hectic family mornings, drawing on the insights of philosopher Pierre Vesperini and the solutions proposed by feminist author Titiou Lecoq.

headline: In Algeria, the pope follows in the footsteps of the country's Christian history and reaches out to Muslims
subhead: On Monday, Leo XIV will become the first pontiff in history to visit Algeria, birthplace of the renowned Christian philosopher Saint Augustine. It was in Hippo, now Annaba, that the theologian wrote his most significant works in the fourth century.
